channel.title: OpenInstead Articles
channel.description: Long-form essays on self-hosting, open source evaluation, and SaaS migration.

item.title: When self-hosting goes wrong: seven failure modes and how to avoid them
item.description: An honest retrospective on the ways self-hosted setups break — not in theory, but in practice — and the small habits that prevent most of them.

item.title: Will the open source project you depend on still exist in three years?
item.description: Bus factor, maintainer burnout, funding models, and the signals that separate OSS projects that survive from those that quietly decay.

item.title: From SaaS to self-hosted: a 30-day migration playbook
item.description: A week-by-week plan to move one service off SaaS and onto your own server without breaking your team's workflow.

item.title: Why the best open source alternative is sometimes a smaller one
item.description: The most-starred project isn't always the right one. A guide to when a smaller, leaner open source tool beats the popular choice.

item.title: Open source alternatives: the comparison criteria that actually matter
item.description: GitHub stars lie. Forks can mislead. Here's a framework for evaluating open source projects that goes beyond the vanity metrics.

item.title: The real monthly cost of a Notion workspace at scale
item.description: Add-ons, seats, AI credits, storage. What a mid-sized team actually pays for Notion once you count everything — and what switching saves.

item.title: Self-hosting in 2026: the honest survey
item.description: An honest look at what self-hosting actually costs in 2026 — time, money, attention — and who should and shouldn't do it.

item.title: Why your team probably can't ditch Slack yet (and what needs to change)
item.description: Mattermost and Rocket.Chat are excellent. So why do most teams who try to migrate away from Slack end up back on Slack? An honest look at the real blockers.
